1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
8		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
9	)
10	
11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	By default work submits to the running ddx server as a background worker and
37	returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current process.
38	`,
39			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
40	  ddx work
41	
42	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
43	  ddx work --local --once
44	
45	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
46	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
47	
48	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
49	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
50	
51	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
52	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
53			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
54			RunE: f.runWork,
55		}
56	
57		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
58		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
59		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
60		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
61		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
63		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
64		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
65		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
66		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
67		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
68		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
69		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Run inline in current process instead of server worker (default: submit to server)")
71		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")
72		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
74		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
75		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
76		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
77		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
78		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
79		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
80	
81		return cmd
82	}
83	
84	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
85	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
86	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
87	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
88	// by this layer.
89	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
90		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true)
91	}
92

1573		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6		"sync/atomic"
7		"testing"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
12		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
14		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
15	)
16	
17	// TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue covers ddx-dc157075 AC #2: with
18	// poll-interval > 0, the loop must NOT exit when nextCandidate returns no
19	// eligible bead. It must reset the per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps and
20	// sleep, then poll again. Cancelling the context is the only way out.
21	func TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue(t *testing.T) {
22		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
23		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
24	
25		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
26			Store: store,
27			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
28				t.Fatalf("executor must not run on an empty queue")
29				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
30			}),
31		}
32	
33		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
34		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
35	
36		// Use a tiny poll interval so the test runs quickly; cancel after enough
37		// wall-clock for several empty-poll cycles to confirm the loop is still
38		// running and not bailing out after the first empty poll.
39		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
40		pollInterval := 5 * time.Millisecond
41		cancelAfter := 50 * time.Millisecond
42		go func() {
43			time.Sleep(cancelAfter)
44			cancel()
45		}()
46	
47		start := time.Now()
48		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
49			PollInterval: pollInterval,
50		})
51		elapsed := time.Since(start)
52	
53		// Context cancellation surfaces as the returned error. The key
54		// invariant: the loop survived past the first empty poll.
55		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
56		require.NotNil(t, result)
57		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
58		assert.True(t, elapsed >= cancelAfter,
59			"loop must run until ctx cancellation; ran for %s, expected >= %s", elapsed, cancelAfter)
60	}
61	
62	// TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues covers ddx-dc157075 AC #3: when the
63	// routing preflight rejects the first ready bead, the loop must NOT exit —
64	// it must record the worker-level failure and proceed to the next ready bead.
65	// Without the fix the loop returns early after the first preflight failure
66	// and the second bead is silently abandoned.
67	func TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues(t *testing.T) {
68		store, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
69	
70		var executed []string
71		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
72			Store: store,
73			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
74				executed = append(executed, beadID)
75				return ExecuteBeadReport{
76					BeadID:    beadID,
77					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
78					SessionID: "sess-after-reject",
79					ResultRev: "deadbeef",
80				}, nil
81			}),
82		}
83	
84		// Preflight rejects only the first bead; the second is allowed through.
85		var preflightCalls int32
86		preflight := func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
87			n := atomic.AddInt32(&preflightCalls, 1)
88			if n == 1 {
89				return agentlib.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible{
90					Harness:         harness,
91					Model:           model,
92					SupportedModels: []string{"x"},
93				}
94			}
95			return nil
96		}
97	
98		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker", Harness: "claude", Model: "gpt-5"}
99		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
100	
101		// PollInterval=0 so the loop drains current ready work and exits when no
102		// more candidates remain — we just need to prove it didn't bail on bead #1.
103		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
104			RoutePreflight: preflight,
105		})
106		require.NoError(t, err)
107		require.NotNil(t, result)
108	
109		// Both beads were considered; the second was actually executed.
110		assert.Equal(t, int32(2), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightCalls),
111			"preflight must be invoked for both candidates (rejection of #1 cannot abandon #2)")
112		require.Len(t, executed, 1, "second bead must run once preflight passes for it")
113		assert.Equal(t, second.ID, executed[0])
114	
115		// First bead is recorded as a worker-level failure.
116		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(result.Results), 2)
117		var firstReport, secondReport *ExecuteBeadReport
118		for i := range result.Results {
119			switch result.Results[i].BeadID {
120			case first.ID:
121				firstReport = &result.Results[i]
122			case second.ID:
123				secondReport = &result.Results[i]
124			}
125		}
126		require.NotNil(t, firstReport, "first bead's failure record present")
127		require.NotNil(t, secondReport, "second bead's success record present")
128		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, firstReport.Status)
129		assert.True(t, strings.Contains(firstReport.Detail, "preflight"),
130			"first bead failure detail must mention preflight; got %q", firstReport.Detail)
131		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, secondReport.Status)
132	
133		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
134		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
135	
136		// First bead must remain open (no claim, no executor invocation).
137		gotFirst, err := store.Get(first.ID)
138		require.NoError(t, err)
139		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotFirst.Status)
140	}
141	
142	// TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits covers ddx-dc157075 back-compat: --once must
143	// still terminate the loop after exactly one ready bead is processed, even
144	// when more beads are queue-ready. This guards against an over-eager fix
145	// that drops the Once gate.
146	func TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits(t *testing.T) {
147		store, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
148	
149		var executed []string
150		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
151			Store: store,
152			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
153				executed = append(executed, beadID)
154				return ExecuteBeadReport{
155					BeadID:    beadID,
156					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
157					SessionID: "sess-once",
158					ResultRev: "cafebabe",
159				}, nil
160			}),
161		}
162	
163		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
164		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
165	
166		// Even with poll-interval > 0 (the new long-running default), --once must
167		// still cause the loop to exit after one bead.
168		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
169			Once:         true,
170			PollInterval: 30 * time.Second,
171		})
172		require.NoError(t, err)
173		require.NotNil(t, result)
174	
175		assert.Len(t, executed, 1, "--once must process exactly one bead")
176		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
177		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
178	}
179	
180	// TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits covers ddx-dc157075 back-compat: an explicit
181	// --poll-interval=0 (legacy "drain-and-exit" semantics) must still exit when
182	// the queue is empty, even though the new default is 30s.
183	func TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits(t *testing.T) {
184		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
185		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
186	
187		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
188			Store: store,
189			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
190				t.Fatalf("executor must not run when queue is empty")
191				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
192			}),
193		}
194	
195		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
196		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
197	
198		// PollInterval=0 explicitly: exit when queue is empty rather than poll.
199		// A bounded-time context guards against a regression that would hang.
200		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Second)
201		defer cancel()
202	
203		start := time.Now()
204		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
205			PollInterval: 0,
206		})
207		elapsed := time.Since(start)
208	
209		require.NoError(t, err, "explicit poll-interval=0 must return cleanly without timing out")
210		require.NotNil(t, result)
211		assert.True(t, elapsed < time.Second,
212			"explicit poll=0 must exit promptly when queue is empty; took %s", elapsed)
213		assert.True(t, result.NoReadyWork)
214		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
215	}
216

input.content:
No code changes required — every acceptance criterion for ddx-dc157075 is
already satisfied in this worktree's base revision (c8841f99). Verified
each AC by reading the cited files and running the targeted tests.

(a) What is done

  AC #1 — Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites:
    - cli/cmd/work.go:68            (30*time.Second, with help text noting opt-out)
    - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568     (30*time.Second, same help text)
    - cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:118 (pollInterval := 30 * time.Second)
    - cli/internal/server/server.go:2328          (pollInterval := 30 * time.Second)

  AC #2 — execute_bead_loop.go: when nextCandidate returns no eligible
    bead AND poll-interval > 0, the loop now emits loop.idle, resets the
    per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps, sleeps, and continues
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:492-559). The early-return
    path only fires for the explicit opt-out (--once or
    --poll-interval=0).

  AC #3 — Routing preflight rejection is per-bead `continue`, not a loop
    exit (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:608-651). The bead is
    recorded as a worker-level failure, and the loop proceeds to the
    next candidate (still honouring --once when set).

  AC #4 — loop.end event includes a structured exit_reason field
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:432-441). Recognized values
    populated in this loop: "sigint", "sigterm", "fatal_config",
    "once_complete", "explicit_poll_zero", "context_cancelled". The
    "providers_exhausted" slot is reserved (comment at lines 428-431)
    for ddx-aede917d.

  AC #5 — Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as a transient
    "running (idle)" substate. The loop emits a loop.idle event +
    progress event with Phase="loop.idle"
    (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:517-533). The server
    progress consumer maps this to WorkerRecord.Substate = "idle"
    (cli/internal/server/workers.go:1360-1371) and clears it on the
    next active phase (workers.go:852, execute_bead_loop.go:564-572).
    The terminal "exited" status is reserved for true exits.

  AC #6 — All four tests exist and pass:
    - TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue
    - TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues
    - TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits
    - TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits
    Located in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go.
    Verified passing locally:
      go test -run 'TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue|TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues|TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits|TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits' ./internal/agent/
      → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.124s

  AC #8 — FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 docs both note the new long-running
    default and opt-out flags:
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:44-47
    - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:247-257

  AC #7 (manual verification) — Not re-run in this pass; the behaviour
    is fully covered by TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (loop survives
    past the first empty poll until ctx cancellation).

(b) What is blocking

  Nothing. The only diff in the worktree is an unrelated deletion of
  .agents/skills/sample-skill, which is outside this bead's scope.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need

  Nothing for this bead. If the post-merge reviewer disagrees with any
  AC mapping above, the fix is to surface the specific gap — every code
  path cited is currently in the tree.

data.text:
All AC items for ddx-dc157075 are already implemented in the worktree base (c8841f99). Verified by reading the cited files and running the four target tests — all pass.

- AC #1: 30s default at all four call sites (work.go:68, agent_cmd.go:1568, graphql_adapters.go:118, server.go:2328)
- AC #2/#3: `execute_bead_loop.go` already loops on empty queue (492-559), resets attempted/hookFailed maps before sleep, and continues per-bead on preflight rejection (608-651)
- AC #4: `loop.end` emits structured `exit_reason` (432-441)
- AC #5: `loop.idle` event drives `WorkerRecord.Substate = "idle"` in `workers.go:1360-1371`
- AC #6: All four tests present and passing in `execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go`
- AC #8: FEAT-006:44-47 and FEAT-010:247-257 both document the new default

Wrote rationale to `.ddx/executions/20260503T025612-a3a8cb42/no_changes_rationale.txt`. No commit — nothing to change.
